name: Central African Republic

visa_free.notes: Visa-free under CEMAC free movement agreement (effective October 31, 2017); national ID card acceptable in lieu of passport

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival established October 2019 per UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs; must be pre-arranged at a CAR diplomatic mission abroad before travel — confirmed by evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ ('A visa on arrival can be obtained only if it was pre-arranged at a CAR diplomatic mission abroad'). The evisa FAQ lists UAE in the visa-exempt exceptions list but clarifies that VoA requires pre-arrangement; max stay duration not specified on any accessible official source.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not in visa-free list
e_visa.notes: Tourist e-visa available via evisa-centrafrique.com (operated by DigitalGate SARL, a third-party referencing the CAR Ministry of Foreign Affairs). Only tourist purpose (individual and group). Cannot be extended inside or outside CAR. Visas from other African nations are NOT accepted for CAR entry. Visa on arrival is only available if pre-arranged at a CAR diplomatic mission abroad. As of 2025-2026, the e-visa portal is operating in 'testing mode' and electronic visa processing is currently not possible per the portal notice. Entry via e-visa is only permitted at Bangui city airport. Stay duration: 1 to 180 days per FAQ.

visa_policy.notes: The official CAR MFA domain (diplomatie.gouv.cf) uses a self-signed SSL certificate preventing content extraction. The CAR Embassy in Washington (usrcaembassy.org) states 'All travelers must apply for a visa for entry into the Central African Republic'; fee schedule listed (short stay 0-1 month $150, 45-day $175, 2-3 month $250, 3+ month $300, express +$50). Official/diplomatic passport holders: 'No change. Visa applications will be processed by the Embassy' — no exemption for diplomatic/official passports. Effective February 12, 2024, embassy/international organization staff (UN, EU, NGOs, embassy contractors) must obtain prior MFA authorization before entering CAR. The US visa exemption was cancelled effective November 17, 2023 per Wikipedia (confirmed by US Embassy Bangui alerts). The evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ (which references CAR Ministry of Foreign Affairs) lists 17 visa-exempt nationalities — this differs from Wikipedia's 14-country list by adding DRC, Nigeria, and UAE (UAE is treated as VoA with pre-arrangement). The portal is operated by DigitalGate SARL and is currently in testing mode with e-visa processing unavailable.

cbi.notes: No active official Citizenship-by-Investment program exists as of 2026. The government proposed a crypto-based CBI program via the Sango Project (USD 60,000 in Sango Coins locked for 5 years for citizenship; USD 6,000 locked for 3 years for e-residency). The Constitutional Court ruled this unconstitutional, holding that 'nationality has no market value' and residency can only be conferred on those physically present in the country. The Sango Coin program is not active.

agreements: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) free movement of persons — member states Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Republic of Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon; agreement signed 2013, ratified and effective October 31, 2017
ECCAS (Economic Community of Central African States) — Protocol on Freedom of Movement adopted 1983; broader regional framework with limited practical implementation beyond CEMAC sub-bloc

validation_notes: ADDED DRC (COD) to visa_free: evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ (which references CAR MFA) explicitly lists Democratic Republic of the Congo as visa-exempt; it was previously omitted per hard rules (not in Wikipedia's 14-country list), but the MFA-referencing portal is a stronger signal than Wikipedia aggregation
ADDED Nigeria (NGA) to visa_free: same source as DRC — evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ lists Nigeria as visa-exempt; previously omitted per prior hard rules

REMOVED USA from visa_free: the US exemption was cancelled November 17, 2023; evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ still lists USA as exempt but this contradicts the Wikipedia policy article's recorded change and the US Embassy in CAR's alert; the usrcaembassy.org states 'All travelers must apply for a visa'; USA removed from visa_free as the exemption was revoked — the evisa portal listing appears outdated/not yet updated
UPDATED e_visa max_stay_days: FAQ confirms stay duration of 1 to 180 days; previously listed as null
UPDATED e_visa notes: added that the portal is currently in 'testing mode' and 'Electronic visa processing is currently not possible' per the live portal notice as of 2025-2026
UPDATED visa_on_arrival UAE notes: confirmed the VoA pre-arrangement requirement from the evisa FAQ directly

CONFIRMED conditional_access remains empty: usrcaembassy.org explicitly states diplomatic/official passport holders still require visas ('No change. Visa applications will be processed by the Embassy'); no credential-based or special-status exemptions found from any official source
CONFIRMED cbi has_program=false: Sango Project ruled unconstitutional by CAR Constitutional Court; no active program

RETAINED Niger (NER) in visa_free: present in Wikipedia's 14-country list but NOT in evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ's 17-country list — discrepancy flagged in notes; retained with updated source and gap notice
SOURCE UPDATE: updated source_url for most visa_free entries from Wikipedia to evisa-centrafrique.com/faq (which cites CAR MFA), as that is a more authoritative reference for this policy
gaps: diplomatie.gouv.cf (official CAR MFA) uses a self-signed SSL certificate — content not extractable; no official enumeration of visa-exempt nationalities obtained from this source

Visa-free list discrepancy: evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ (MFA-referencing) lists 17 countries including DRC and Nigeria; Wikipedia lists 14 (excluding DRC, Nigeria, UAE from visa-free). Neither is a direct CAR government (.gouv.cf) page.

USA visa exemption: evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ still lists USA as exempt, contradicting the November 17, 2023 cancellation recorded on Wikipedia and confirmed by US Embassy alerts. Status of US exemption is ambiguous from the evisa portal (which may not be updated). USA excluded from visa_free based on the more authoritative November 2023 cancellation record.
Niger status: present in Wikipedia's 14-country list but absent from the evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ's 17-country list — unclear which is correct.
max_stay_days for UAE visa on arrival: not specified on any accessible official source.
US Embassy in CAR alert pages (cf.usembassy.gov) returned Technical Difficulties — content not extractable.
E-visa portal currently in 'testing mode' with processing unavailable — it is unclear when or if the full e-visa system will become operational; no official MFA announcement found.
No official CAR government page listing visa fees verified from .gouv.cf domain (fees from usrcaembassy.org, the CAR Embassy in the US).
No official government source found for RBI, digital nomad, fast-track, or talent programs.
No conditional_access rules (credential-based, special-status, or diplomatic-passport-type waivers) confirmed from any official CAR source.

outbound_official.note: No official Central African Republic government outbound visa-list page located for ordinary passport holders. The CAR Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (diplomatie.gouv.cf) is live and fully scraped: its 'Voyage en Centrafrique' service page only references visas for foreigners entering CAR and diplomatic visa procedures for officials travelling abroad — no comprehensive, publicly published visa-free/VOA/e-visa destination list for ordinary CAR passport holders exists on any .gouv.cf page. The 'Conseil aux voyageurs' page only addresses COVID-era health measures for travellers. No such outbound list was found on paris.diplomatie.gouv.cf or any other official CAR government domain. Rely on destination-side data.

Visa applications will be processed by the Embassy'); provides fee schedule; NGO/international organization staff require prior MFA authorization; no credential-based entry rules found

FAQ page of the CAR MFA-referencing e-visa portal; lists 17 visa-exempt nationalities (Benin, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Gabon, DRC, Israel, Cameroon, Côte d'Ivoire, Liberia, Nigeria, UAE, Republic of the Congo, Rwanda, Senegal, USA, Chad, Equatorial Guinea); confirms VoA requires pre-arrangement; confirms e-visa entry only at Bangui airport; stay up to 180 days
